nativeRange.habitat: Stagnant waters and swampy regions of tropical and subtropical South America; typically encountered as a submerged stem plant in slow-moving or still bodies of water.

decorPreferences: nutrient-rich substrate preferred (aquasoil) but tolerates inert gravel with root tabs
open midground or background space to allow bushy lateral branching
best planted as a dense group of stems for a fine, feathery effect

avoidWith: plant-eating fish such as goldfish, silver dollars, and large herbivorous cichlids
boisterous diggers that uproot delicate stems

summary: Heteranthera zosterifolia (Star Grass) is a fast-growing South American stem plant with fine, narrow, bright green leaves arranged in whorls around the stem. Generally advised for the midground or background, where it forms a feathery, bushy thicket and is well known for producing a striking pearling effect under strong light with CO2.
careNotes: Heteranthera zosterifolia is a popular South American stem plant native to tropical and subtropical regions including Brazil, Argentina, Paraguay, Uruguay, and Bolivia, where it grows in stagnant waters and swampy areas. It is generally placed in the midground or background, where its bushy, well-branched habit produces a fine, feathery look. Under stronger light with CO2 injection, the leaves are well known for emitting a stream of small oxygen bubbles — the distinctive pearling effect that makes Star Grass a popular choice for high-tech display tanks. Lighting is commonly considered medium to high; the plant tolerates lower light but will grow leggier and less compact. CO2 is not strictly required but is strongly recommended for the densest growth and best coloration — Flowgrow notes a beneficial range of roughly 15-40 mg/L. Temperature tolerance is broad (sources span roughly 10-30°C); the 20-28°C window above reflects the typical tropical aquarium range. pH tolerance is also wide (Flowgrow reports 5-8); 5.5-7.5 captures the common consensus. Nutrient-rich substrate and regular column dosing (nitrate, phosphate, potassium, iron, and trace elements) keep the bright green color and prevent leaf shedding from the lower stems. Aquarium Co-Op notes that 'normal underwater grown stargrass is difficult to ship,' and tissue-cultured specimens are often offered for that reason. Minimum tank volume is not stated by either confirming source; 60 L is a conservative estimate suitable for a small dense group of stems with room for lateral branching. Cross-source notes: Aquarium Co-Op replaced the originally specified Buce Plant primary (which returned 404 at fetch time); Flowgrow served as the secondary cross-check and provided most of the precise parameter ranges, taxonomy, and native range.
breedingNotes: Propagation is straightforward via stem cuttings: trim a healthy lateral side-shoot or top section and replant directly into the substrate, where it roots readily. Regular topping and replanting maintains a dense, bushy stand and encourages branching from the base; left untrimmed, the plant tends to grow tall and shed lower leaves.

primary.name: Aquarium Co-Op
primary.notes: Used as primary after the assigned Buce Plant URL returned HTTP 404. Aquarium Co-Op confirms identity (Heteranthera zosterifolia / Star Grass), moderate-to-high light, CO2 recommended, fast growth, and tissue-culture availability.

additional.name: Flowgrow Aquatic Plant Database
additional.notes: Secondary cross-source. Supplied taxonomy (Pontederiaceae, Commelinales), native range (tropical/southern South America, Brazil), parameter ranges (temperature 10-30°C with optimum 20-25°C; pH 5-8; kH 0-21 dKH; CO2 15-40 mg/L), fast growth, medium-to-high light, midground/background placement, propagation by cuttings, and easy-to-intermediate difficulty.
